manifest.title: Cursor Cloud Agent Worker
manifest.author: Benn Eichhorn
manifest.tagline: Self-hosted Cursor Cloud Agent worker on your Cloudron
manifest.description:
# Cursor Cloud Agent Worker for Cloudron

Run a [Cursor Cloud Agent](https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/my-machines) worker on your Cloudron server so agents execute in your environment—your repos, tools, and network—while Cursor handles orchestration.

**Who this is for:** Cursor Pro or Personal with self-hosted cloud agents; **My Machines** workers (not Enterprise pools). One app install = one named worker.

**What you get:** Cursor CLI, Node.js, git, build tools, Playwright/Chromium, admin UI at `/admin` (Cloudron login), persistent `/app/data`.
manifest.changelog:
* Admin UI shows Git PAT permission errors with fix instructions
* Worker heartbeat codes for git auth failures
* Link to GitHub personal access token settings in /admin
manifest.postInstallMessage:
## First steps

1. Enable **self-hosted cloud agents** in your [Cursor Cloud Agents dashboard](https://cursor.com/agents).
2. Open **Configure** (`/admin`) and sign in with your Cloudron account.
3. **Connect with Cursor** (browser) or paste an **API key** under Advanced.
4. Set repository URL, branch/ref, optional git token, and worker name; save.
5. Pick this machine at [cursor.com/agents](https://cursor.com/agents).

* Fix git HOME on persistent volume
* Admin connect shows device-flow link reliably
* API key status no longer requires agent in PATH

* Fat image: Cursor CLI, Node 20, Playwright/Chromium, build tools
